title: IMD Issues Rain, Thunderstorm and Heatwave Alert for Telangana and AP for Next 3 Days - Munsif News 24x7
full_text:
Hyderabad: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an important weather update for Telangana and Andhra Pradesh, warning of rain, thunderstorms, gusty winds and heatwave conditions over the next three days. While some areas are witnessing scorching heat, several districts are also expected to receive light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning.
Table of Contents
According to the Amaravati Meteorological Centre and Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, changing climatic conditions are continuing across the Telugu states.
Monsoon Conditions Favourable Over Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea
The Amaravati Meteorological Centre stated that the northern limit of the monsoon trough currently passes through:
The department said conditions are favourable for further advance of the Southwest Monsoon into more parts of the Southeast Arabian Sea, Southwest Bay of Bengal, Andaman Islands, remaining parts of the Andaman Sea and some parts of Eastcentral Bay of Bengal during the next 2-3 days.
The IMD also stated that the onset of Southwest Monsoon over Kerala is likely around May 26, 2026, with a variation of four days earlier or later.
Cyclonic Circulation and Trough Continue to Influence Weather
The cyclonic circulation over southwest Bay of Bengal and adjoining Tamil Nadu has now shifted over northeast Tamil Nadu and nearby regions at 1.5 km above mean sea level.
Meanwhile, the trough extending from west Vidarbha through Marathwada, Interior Karnataka and Tamil Nadu across the Gulf of Mannar at 0.9 km above mean sea level continues to persist.
These systems are influencing weather conditions across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.
Andhra Pradesh Weather Forecast for Next 3 Days
North Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Yanam
Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are likely at one or two places on Sunday, Monday and Tuesday.
Thunderstorms and gusty winds with speeds of 30-40 kmph are also expected.
South Coastal Andhra Pradesh
Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are likely at one or two places on Sunday and Monday with thunderstorms and gusty winds of 30-40 kmph.
On Tuesday, light to moderate rain or thundershowers are expected with thunderstorms and gusty winds reaching 40-50 kmph.
Rayalaseema
Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are likely at one or two places on Sunday and Monday along with thunderstorms and gusty winds of 30-40 kmph.
On Tuesday, thunderstorms and gusty winds with speeds of 40-50 kmph are likely along with rainfall.
Temperature Forecast for Andhra Pradesh
The IMD stated that there will be no significant change in maximum temperatures over Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Yanam during the next four days.
However, temperatures are likely to gradually rise by 2-3 degrees Celsius over the next three days.
No major change in maximum temperatures is expected over Rayalaseema during the next five days.
Telangana Weather Forecast and Heatwave Warning
According to the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, the low-pressure area over southwest Bay of Bengal weakened on Saturday evening.
For Telangana, light to moderate rain is likely at isolated places over one or two districts on Sunday.
Dry weather is expected across the state on Monday and Tuesday.
The maximum temperatures across Telangana are likely to gradually rise by 2-3 degrees Celsius during the next three days.
Heatwave Alert for Northern Telangana Districts
The IMD warned that heatwave conditions are very likely in some northern districts of Telangana today, tomorrow and the day after tomorrow.
The department also warned that light to moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds with speeds of 30-40 kmph may occur at isolated places in some districts of the state today.
The India Meteorological Department has issued a three-day weather alert for Telangana and Andhra Pradesh warning of thunderstorms, rain, gusty winds and heatwave conditions. While several districts are likely to witness light to moderate rainfall with winds reaching up to 50 kmph, temperatures are also expected to rise by 2-3 degrees Celsius in many areas. The IMD has advised people to remain cautious as changing weather conditions continue across the Telugu states.

title: Heatwave Conditions Likely in North Telangana Districts This Week - Deccan Chronicle
full_text:
The IMD cautioned that high temperatures could increase the risk of heat-related illnesses, particularly among infants, elderly persons and those with chronic diseases
Hyderabad: North Telangana, where the mercury has crossed the 45ºC mark, is expected to get hotter over the next five days, with maximum temperature expected to rise by 2°C to 3°C, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad.
Hyderabad is likely to record maximum temperature between 40°C and 41°C over the next 48 hours. The IMD cautioned that high temperatures could increase the risk of heat-related illnesses, particularly among infants, elderly persons and those with chronic diseases.

title: Telangana braces for severe heatwave as IMD issues seven-day alert - Mid-day
full_text:
Heatwave warning for Telangana for next seven days
The IMD has issued a heatwave warning for parts of Telangana over the next seven days, with temperatures likely to cross 45°C in several districts. Authorities have issued health advisories and strengthened preparedness at public health facilities
Representational Image/Pixabay
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave warning for the next seven days in parts of Telangana.
The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre has forecast a gradual rise in the maximum temperature by 2-3 degrees Celsius during the next three days in Telangana.

title: Telangana braces for severe heatwave as IMD issues red and orange alerts till May 21 - Hyderabad Mail
full_text:
HYDERABAD: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued red and orange heatwave alerts across Telangana, warning that severe summer conditions are likely to continue till May 21.
Several districts have been placed under red alert, with temperatures expected to cross 45°C. Most other districts are under orange alert, where maximum temperatures are likely to remain between 41°C and 44°C.
A prolonged spell of dry heat has gripped the state, pushing all districts into a high-temperature zone and triggering concerns over public health and safety.
Health officials said the prevailing conditions could increase the risk of heat exhaustion, dehydration and heatstroke, particularly among infants, elderly persons and people with chronic illnesses.
The Director of Public Health (DPH), in a heatwave advisory, urged people to avoid outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours. Officials advised residents to wear light cotton clothing and maintain adequate hydration by consuming water, oral rehydration solution (ORS) and traditional fluids.
#Heatwave Alert:
The #IMD has issued an #OrangeAlert for maximum districts and #RedAlert for some districts in #Telangana, #Warning of a #Severe #Heatwaves till May 21.

Authorities also cautioned labourers, outdoor workers and commuters to take extra precautions as temperatures are expected to remain high over the coming days.
